title: Connections, Channels, Heartbeats, Errors, and Recovery · Inference Brief: Recovery Order Generation
summary: A service opens one TCP connection per message, shares one channel across unrelated concurrent owners, disables heartbeats, retries instantly, and redeclares consumers before topology or credentials recover. Planned artifact: Add and verify the recovery order generation increment in connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Recreate connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale callbacks.
Reconnect with bounded exponential backoff, jitter, cancellation, credential refresh, readiness, and a single recovery owner.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Recovery Order Generation · Theory Recovery Order Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8898 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound heartbeat and reconnect policy while rejecting stale channel-generation callbacks; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Recovery Order Generation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Recovery Order Generation · Theory Recovery Order Generation.
